page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs powered by 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the instance supports a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top 25% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks fall into the top 25% tier. Cryptographic capabilities are particularly notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test reaching the top 10% tier. However, single-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tasks remains in the middle 50% average tier.

While the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. LLM inference speed for text generation using a large 70B model falls into the bottom 25% tier, though prompt processing for the same model remains in the average tier. This profile indicates the instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving rather than large-scale LLM text generation. The high density of memory and local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets and high-speed local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place the instance in the top 25% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with cryptographic performance reaching the top 10%. Conversely, single-core benchmarks and large LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. This hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and high-throughput web serving where local NVMe storage and high memory density offer qualitative resource efficiency.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform combining high-capacity DDR4 RAM, local NVMe storage, and 150 Gbps networking for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 memory driven by Intel Xeon 8375C processors
5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top 10% performance in cryptographic encryption benchmarks
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET performance
Weak text generation speeds for large language models
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and web serving
